item.title: Upcoming Halloween Special from my favorite Spooky Band - BROKEN PEACH!
item.description: If you haven't listened to Broken Peach before, go and check them out. Their Halloween Specials are what initially caught my eye and it's been a great ride ever since! They'll be streaming their Halloween Special on Wednesday, 30 Oct! https://www.youtube.com/@TheBrokenPeach/featured

item.title: Fresh(ish) Memes #335
item.dc:creator: ShaggyRoots

item.title: 1 Month Post-Accident Followup
item.description: Well, I finally made it home. House was a bit of a disaster. Healing is progressing nicely according to my doctors, and I should be able to walk again inside a month. Remember to wear a helmet and riding gear. The latter didn't do much to save me from the impact of the car, but it did enough to keep vital juices inside where they belong when I went skidding across the pavement. The helmet did its job, and was sacrificed in the process. It could have been so much worse.
item.dc:creator: misterwilson

item.title: Different kinds of rock formations
item.description:
Robert Plant catches a dove at Kezar Stadium, 1973.
David Bowie photographed by Philippe Auliac in 1976
Flea and Anthony Kiedis photographed by Andy Warhol in Los Angeles, 1985
Soundgarden photographed by Michael Lavine, 1989
John Belushi, Mick Jagger, Steve Martin & Dan Aykroyd (1980)
Trent Reznor photographed by Joseph Cultice
Henry Rollins' first proper show with Black Flag, August 21, 1981, Cuckoo's Nest, Costa Mesa CA
Kurt Cobain and his cat (1989)
Thom Yorke about to get a haircut from Jonny Greenwood. Photographed by Pat Pope (1994)
Ian Curtis photographed by Lex Van Rossen at the Paradiso in Amsterdam, 1980
Elvis Presley in 1969
Robert Plant photographed by Penny Smith in 1974
Johnny Cash, photographed by Dan Poush before going on stage at Folsom Prison in 1968
Depeche Mode photographed by Anton Corbijn, 1997
Kurt Cobain with his pet turtle, photographed by Tracy Marander
Jeff Buckley photographed by Hans van den Bogaard in Amsterdam, 1995
Eddie Vedder's stage dive during a Pearl Jam show, 1992
Stevie Nicks photographed by Chuck Pulin at Madison Square Garden in NY - June 30, 1977
David Bowie and Iman in Cape Town, 1995
A young Nick Cave photographed by Tony Mottram
Suzi Quatro in the 1970s
Alice Cooper and Salvador Dali photographed by Bob Gruen in 1973
Jimi Hendrix in 1968
Nirvana photographed by Renaud Monfourny in 1993
A 26 year old Ozzy Osbourne in 1974
Joan Jett with the Blackhearts - the "Bad Reputation" tour (1982)
item.dc:creator: stardustLUST

item.title: Holy shit, how stupid does CrunchyRoll have to be to do this?
item.description: For the record as most here may be familiar due to election season: tampering with the mail in any way, including theft, is a felony. The USPIS is going to have a field day with this case. Five fucking years of stealing a voice actor's mail. The fuck. Source: https://twitter.com/davidwald_va/status/1849901208104022257 P.S. If anyone from Crunchyroll reads this: the USPS maintains an audit log of each piece of postmarked mail sent to a given address. Those five years of stolen mail is going to be a very easy thing to prove if Mr. Wald did not get any forwarding service. That's a lot of stolen mail, and each offense adds another 5 years of prison time and $250,000 in fines.
item.dc:creator: NKato

item.dc:creator: Comet260

item.title: Oh my god I am DYING
item.description: I’ve had foster kittens Freddy and Jason for a week, and they came to me very scared and very sick. It’s been a long week of worries and vet visits, medication and fluids, running from me and hiding. But I came home for lunch today and THIS happened: the cutest thing I have ever seen!! Freddy was so comfortable in my lap and Jason was more than willing to share the attention. My heart is full!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: She loves to hold hands
item.description: This squirrel is an unreleasable wild animal who was raised by wildlife rehabbers.
item.dc:creator: rhinokitty

item.title: Author dedications are an art unto themselves.
item.description: MV Edit - I haven't published a novel yet but I did dedicate a short story to one person after their random act of kindness (https://imgur.com/gallery/im-still-processing-this-one-of-nicest-things-anyones-ever-done-me-64Fsg2i). I think if I *did* do a dedication, I'd include Imgur since it's still one of those die-hard-rascals-with-a-heart-of-gold communities that I used to inhabit since the early 00s. Anyway, if you're interested in being a part of that dedication, here's one of the novel projects I'm working on - https://inkican.com/mike-sierra-echo-middle-grade-scifi-space-novel-portal/ - help me talk to a lit agent that specializes in scifi and middle-grade fiction. Connecting me is cheaper than buying me a pizza - and it would legit change my life and the life of my rescue kitties.
item.dc:creator: inkican

item.title: RIP Jeri Taylor, writer for "Star Trek: The Next Generation" and co-creator of "Star Trek: Voyager". A sci-fi legend sadly passed away at the age of 86.
item.description:
Taylor’s career before Trek began in the late 1970s (writing episodes of "Little House on the Prairie" and "The Incredible Hulk"), eventually becoming a staff writer and producer on the drama "Quincy M.E" in 1980, where she met her second husband, David Moessigner. She worked as a writer and producer on several other popular series during the 80s, including "Magnum P.I.", "In the Heat of the Night" and "Jake and the Fatman" in 1989, her first credit as a co-executive producer.
She started her time with TNG as a supervising producer in season 4, with writing credits on several episodes in her first season including sole credit on the critically acclaimed episode “The Drumhead.” Other great episodes of hers include "The Wounded", "The Outcast", "Night Terrors" and "Unification".
Taylor is likely most associated for her work on Voyager, including writing credits for 14 episodes, starting with the series premiere “Caretaker.” Developing the character of Captain Kathryn Janeway was particularly important to Taylor. [Here she's pictured on the set of Voyager with her co-creators Michael Piller and Rick Berman]
Taylor (at a speaking event in 2021) discussed it: “I always thought of Janeway is just me, I wrote her. I can’t say that I wrote her as me, but as I would like to be. I felt full of her. And I think she is full of me, although, in many ways, far more skilled and able than I am. But it was my fantasy that Janeway was me and I tried to approach it like that. Flaws—we all have flaws. I could name my own; I don’t know that I could name Janeway’s” She even gave Janeway the same hometown as her own - Bloomington, Indiana
Trek historian Larry Nemecek pictured with her in the late 90s
Pictured with Michael Okuda, a key artistic designer for Star Trek Okuda: "The world of Star Trek lost one of its giants with the passing of writer-producer Jeri Taylor. Jeri became a supervising producer on Star Trek: TNG’s fourth season, eventually becoming co-executive producer. Along with Rick Berman and Michael Piller, she co-created Star Trek: Voyager, on which she served as executive producer. Jeri brought humanity and compassion to her work and to Star Trek. Her scripts include “The Drumhead” (TNG), which remains one of Star Trek’s most powerful hours." "Working for Jeri Taylor was always a delight. She always welcomed ideas and suggestions, even from designers in the art department. She was thoughtful and warm, and she always made us feel like we were an important part of her team."
Bryan Fuller: O, CAPTAIN, MY CAPTAIN
Pictured in a writers' room meeting with Brannon Braga and Bryan Fuller Brannon: "She was a great writer and showrunner and, to me, a cherished mentor. Jeri was generous with her wisdom and her time, she nurtured an entire staff of young writers which is a testament to her patience. I would not have a career without Jeri’s intricate guidance. She taught us all so much. Her memory will live on in many ways, but perhaps most of all in the character of Captain Janeway, who reflected the best dimensions of Jeri herself. Jeri Taylor, we were lucky to know you."
33 years ago Gene Roddenberry passed away on the same day (Oct 24th, 1991) While Roddenberry may get all the credit for Star Trek's success, it was women like Taylor throughout the franchise's history that truely defined it as great sci-fi television.
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: My heart! I honestly don't know why this touched me so much...but the smallest gestures of kindness really can mean the world! I hope their kid is ok now!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: Well now you’re just being a diva, Freddy
item.description: Foster kitten Freddy has only just today realized that he loooooooves pets and attention. Up until this afternoon he wanted nothing to do with me. But now he won’t shut up. Good thing I love him very much. Little brother Jason is just going to have to get used to sharing my love.
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Best lunch break ever
item.description: I popped home at lunch to give foster kittens Jason and Freddy some wet food and their coccidia medication. Freddy has been VERY standoffish but look at him now! I’m careful not to touch him too much, he’s still getting used to being close to me. They are both doing so well. And their little meows are killing me!!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays
